preamble: The shift from the voiced bilabial stop [b] to the voiced labio-velar approximant [w] is a common form of lenition, particularly in intervocalic positions where the stop closure weakens into a glide.

examples.note: Intervocalic /b/ spirantizes to [β], which often approaches a glide [w] in rapid or relaxed speech.

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
